channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Rev Jesse Jackson, civil rights leader and Rainbow PUSH founder, dies at 84
item.description: The Rev Jesse Jackson, a prominent civil rights activist and two-time Democratic presidential candidate, died Tuesday morning at age 84, according to ...

item.title: AI could spark a new age of learning, but only if governments, tech firms and educators work together
item.description: Former European Commission president José Manuel Barroso and Efekta Education CEO Stephen Hodges on how AI can help resource-strained education system...

item.title: Tom Emmer blasts Democrats' double standard on SAVE Act: 'They require photo IDs' at their own DNC
item.description: House GOP Whip Tom Emmer blasts Democrats over photo ID rules at the DNC, arguing their stance undercuts their opposition to the SAVE Act and exposes ...

item.title: Thune guarantees voter ID bill to hit the Senate despite Schumer, Dem opposition: 'We will have a vote'
item.description: SAVE America Act faces Senate filibuster hurdle despite 50 GOP backers as Republicans weigh talking filibuster option to advance voter ID requirements...

item.title: New U.S. legislation could upend credit card loyalty programs. The Points Guy founder calls the reckoning un-American
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: Trump says 'this is a Democrat shutdown' as he touts low inflation, falling murder rate
item.description: President Donald Trump blamed Democrats for the partial government shutdown while claiming economic wins including lower gas prices and inflation.

item.title: Cuba Faces a Sunset of Its Socialist Revolution
item.description: Postwar Vietnam is a good model for how a nation can emerge from collectivist tyranny.

item.title: Swalwell's 'I should be working' gym, pool videos resurface as Dem rival hammers his missed House votes
item.description: Rep. Eric Swalwell's pool and gym videos are resurfacing in California's governor race as his opponent's campaign hammers him for missing votes on Cap...

item.title: Rooting Against Anti-American Olympians Is The Patriotic Thing To Do
item.description: U.S. athletes who use the Olympics as an avenue to thumb their noses at America deserve nothing but scorn and defeat.

item.title: Judge orders Park Service to restore slavery exhibits and compares Trump to Orwell's '1984'
item.description: A federal judge ordered the National Park Service to restore mentions of slavery to a park site associated with George Washington in Philadelphia, sco...

item.title: Prosecutors plan to charge an Israeli settler with killing a Palestinian activist in the West Bank
item.description: Israeli prosecutors said Monday that they plan to charge a settler in the killing of a Palestinian activist during a confrontation that was caught on ...

item.title: Outside groups descend on Virginia for redistricting referendum
item.description: A liberal dark money organization has launched a $5 million campaign to persuade voters to back the Democrats' redrawn congressional map in an April r...

item.title: Amazon’s Ring decides maybe partnering with a police surveillance firm is a bad idea after wide revulsion at Super Bowl ad
item.description: The Super Bowl ad depicts a lost dog being tracked by cameras throughout a neighborhood using artificial intelligence.

item.title: Navigating the GOP Divide over Artificial Intelligence
item.description: The key is to draw a line between AI architecture and AI applications.

item.title: Schumer embraced ID laws to counter fraud in the 1990s, but now calls voter ID 'Jim Crow 2.0'
item.description: A video has emerged of Senate Democratic leader Charles E. Schumer in 1996 talking about the importance of ID checks to prevent welfare fraud by illeg...

item.title: Ed Crane Was Right About Free Speech And The Iraq War Years Before The Country Caught Up
item.description: Ed Crane had a gift for identifying moments when liberty required an unpopular defense — and history has a way of catching up to him.

item.title: The ultrawealthy have 3 big secrets on reducing taxes including the way they die
item.description: “Assets like stocks can quietly grow for decades and, when they’re inherited, the tax bill often disappears.”

item.title: Top Hollywood screenwriter warns TikTok’s new tool is at the gates: ‘I hate to say it. It’s likely over for us’
item.description: Seedance 2.0, which is only available in China for now, lets users generate high-quality AI videos using simple text prompts.

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Chip Roy Lays Out How Senate Can Vote On SAVE Act Without Nuking Filibuster
item.description: 'We can move the legislation under CURRENT rules without 'nuking' the filibuster,' Roy pointed out.

item.title: 5 Things To Know About The Secret Wisconsin School Trans Bathroom Policy
item.description: Across the country, reliably red communities like this one assume their schools reflect their values. One by one, they’re learning that assumption is ...

item.title: There Is No Such National Holiday as Presidents Day
item.description: George Washington’s birthday should not be a celebration of presidents in general.

item.title: Protectionism Serves K Street and Hurts Main Street
item.description: The lobbying industry has exploded under Trump’s tariff regime.

item.title: Andrew Sullivan Is A Trump Supporter, But As With Most Things Political He Just Doesn’t Know It
item.description: Someone please tell Sullivan that what he wants from the Democrat Party is precisely what's offered by Trump's presidency.

item.title: Trump sends feds to clean up Potomac River sewage spill
item.description: President Trump on Monday directed federal authorities to step in and aid the cleanup efforts of the wastewater spill in the Potomac River.

item.title: Trump Throws In the Towel on Minneapolis Surge
item.description: Ultimately, the battle is about whether our immigration laws can be enforced.

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Bank Risk
item.description: AI is exposing the limits of legacy bank controls, making governance quality critical to resilience and investor confidence. The post AI Is Reshaping ...

item.title: What Makes an Ideal Leveraged Buyout Candidate?
item.description: In competitive PE markets, successful LBOs depend on durable business models, recurring cash flows, defensible market positions, and disciplined targe...

item.title: Rethinking Household Asset Allocation Under Capital Constraints
item.description: Leveraged ETFs aren’t about more risk, they’re about using less capital to achieve a desired risk profile in household portfolios. The post Rethinking...

item.title: Stockholm’s Capital Markets Success: More Than Meatballs
item.description: Stockholm’s capital-market success reflects more than IPOs and PE activity. It offers a case study in how culture, institutions, and incentives shape ...
